items.title: Fix plan status mismatch: P-0051 filter persistence requirement not met in original delivery
items.description:
## What Has Been Done

Investigated and documented the relationship between P-0051 and P-0055 regarding filter persistence.

**Findings:**
1. P-0051 (commit ea0b569) delivered filtering UI and logic but WITHOUT persistence
2. P-0055 (commit 361f785) delivered persistence later as a separate plan
3. P-0051 was marked review/done without meeting its original acceptance criteria

**Resolution:**
Updated both P-0051 and P-0055 descriptions to properly document their relationship:
- P-0051 now acknowledges that persistence was completed by P-0055
- P-0055 now explicitly states that it completes P-0051's persistence requirement
- Both plan descriptions clearly state that together they deliver the complete filtering feature

**Status:**
The filter persistence requirement is now fully implemented and properly documented. P-0051 + P-0055 together satisfy the original requirement: "Filters persist across close/reopen. Default: show all."

No code changes were required - this was a documentation/process fix to properly reflect the work that was already done.

---

## Issue

P-0051 (Add filtering to plan screen) had explicit requirement: "Filters persist across close/reopen. Default: show all." The original delivery (commit ea0b569) only implemented in-memory filter state, not disk persistence.

The requirement was technically met later by P-0055, but P-0051 was marked done/review without fulfilling its original acceptance criteria.

## Impact

- P-0051 should have been blocked until persistence was implemented
- Acceptance criteria says "persist across close/reopen" but initial delivery violated this
- P-0055 (persistence) was created as separate work instead of being part of P-0051

## What Needs Fixing

Review P-0051's final acceptance and either:
1. Mark P-0051 as incomplete and move its original requirements into P-0055, OR
2. Verify P-0051 was actually completed with persistence (check commit ea0b569 fully)

If option 1: verify P-0055 is marked as completing P-0051's original requirements.

items.title: Review fix: scheduled task picker uses wrong CLI API shape and local-time defaults
items.description:
Objective
Fix the P-0086 scheduled tasks UI rework regression found during reviewer pass.

Original Plan Context
P-0086 requested replacing the scheduled task UI with a Vue sidebar section that can view/edit scheduled tasks, show time until execution, create new tasks, and use the already configured CLI picker and configured folder picker. The plan specifically came from a failed save/create workflow: all fields could be filled out, but Save/Create still did not work reliably.

Reviewer Findings
1. The new CLI picker path in renderer/components/sidebar/ScheduledTasksTab.vue assumes window.gamepadCli.configGetCliTypes() returns Array<{ cliType: string }> and maps c.cliType. The actual IPC/preload contract returns string[] from src/electron/ipc/config-handlers.ts -> ConfigLoader.getCliTypes(). Existing app callers and tests also treat it as string[]. As written, the picker receives undefined entries and cannot present the configured CLIs correctly.
2. The create form does not require a selected CLI. canCreate only requires title, prompt, time, and directory, then silently defaults to 'claude-code'. That violates the requested configured-CLI picker workflow and can create tasks for a CLI that is not the user's chosen configured tool.
3. The default datetime-local value is built with nextHour.toISOString().slice(0, 16). datetime-local expects a local wall-clock string, but toISOString() is UTC. In non-UTC timezones this displays/saves the wrong local time and can schedule the task hours away from the intended next hour, or in the past.
4. P-0086 explicitly requested being able to see/edit current scheduled tasks. The delivered UI lists tasks and can cancel pending tasks, but it has no edit path for existing scheduled tasks and no update IPC usage. That part of the plan is not satisfied.

Implementation Notes
- Treat configGetCliTypes() as string[] in ScheduledTasksTab.vue.
- Require selectedCliType in canCreate and remove the hidden fallback to 'claude-code'.
- Format datetime-local defaults using local date/time components, not toISOString().
- Add an edit flow for existing scheduled tasks, or split a clearly linked follow-up if the backend lacks update support.
- Add or update focused tests for ScheduledTasksTab so this API-shape, datetime-local behavior, create validation, and edit availability cannot regress.

Acceptance Criteria
- Opening the CLI picker shows the configured CLI keys/display names from configGetCliTypes().
- Create remains disabled until a configured CLI and configured directory are selected.
- A newly opened create form defaults Scheduled Time to the next local hour shown correctly in the datetime-local field.
- scheduledTaskCreate receives the selected configured CLI, selected configured directory, prompt, and local intended time.
- Existing scheduled tasks can be edited, or there is an explicit linked implementation plan for adding edit/update support if backend work is required.
- Focused component/unit tests cover the picker API shape, create-form validation, local-time default, and edit path.

items.title: Review fix: backup settings must target a selected directory and use documented config format
items.description:
Objective
Fix backup/restore review gaps found after P-0070/P-0083/P-0084/P-0085 were marked done.

Original Plan Context
P-0070 introduced a per-directory plan backup/restore system with rolling-window snapshots and configurable backup schedule/window size. P-0083 added the restore modal and Settings panel integration, including a manual Backup Now button, selectable backup preview, restore notice with timestamp, and error/loading states. P-0084 required tests for scheduler behavior and config validation. P-0085 documented config/plan-backups.yaml and the restore workflow.

Reviewer Findings
1. renderer/components/sidebar/BackupTab.vue implements Settings -> Backup Now by calling configGetWorkingDirs() and backing up only the first configured directory. That is not the current planner directory, not user-selectable, and can silently snapshot the wrong project.
2. The system is documented as configurable via config/plan-backups.yaml, but PlanBackupManager reads/writes that file with JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. JSON is valid YAML as a subset, but the docs describe YAML-style configuration, and hand-edited YAML will fail to load.
3. BackupTab silently ignores create/save failures, so a user cannot tell whether manual backup or config persistence failed.
4. P-0084 required setBackupConfig invalid-range rejection, but src/electron/ipc/plan-backup-handlers.ts passes updates directly to updateConfig and tests only assert pass-through behavior.
5. P-0083 requested snapshot preview details, including timestamp, plan count, age, and diff vs current. BackupRestoreModal only highlights a selected row and does not show a preview/diff panel.
6. P-0083 requested the restore notice include the timestamp, but App.vue currently sets a generic 'Restored from backup' notice.

Implementation Notes
- Make the Settings backup tab choose an explicit configured directory before Backup Now, or route it through the current planner directory when available. Do not default silently to the first configured folder.
- Align the config parser/writer with the documented YAML contract, or update docs/UI wording if the intended persisted format is JSON-in-.yaml.
- Validate backup config ranges at the IPC/manager boundary: maxSnapshots 1-100 and interval 1-168 hours, matching the UI.
- Surface concise error/success state for Backup Now and config save failures.
- Add the missing selected-snapshot preview/diff details, or deliberately revise the plan/docs if diffing is out of scope.
- Include the restored snapshot timestamp in the success notice.
- Add focused tests for the BackupTab/manual backup target, PlanBackupManager config loading format, scheduler interval behavior if still untested, invalid setBackupConfig ranges, and modal preview/notice behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- Manual Backup Now from Settings cannot create a backup for an unintended first configured folder without user visibility/choice.
- A documented config/plan-backups.yaml example can be loaded by PlanBackupManager, or docs explicitly match the persisted format.
- Failed backup creation/config save is visible in the UI.
- Invalid backup config ranges are rejected before persistence.
- BackupRestoreModal shows selected-snapshot preview details, including timestamp, plan count, age, and current-state comparison/diff where feasible.
- Restore success notice identifies the restored snapshot timestamp.
- Tests cover directory target selection, documented config-file format, invalid config ranges, the automatic backup interval gate, preview rendering, and restore notice text.

items.title: 10. Plan attachments with MCP temp-file retrieval
items.description:
Problem Statement
Plans currently contain title/description/type/status/dependency metadata but cannot carry attachments. Users need to attach JSON, text/code files, images, and arbitrary binary files up to 10MB, store them under the Helm config directory, and access them through MCP tools.

User POV
As a user, I want to attach relevant artifacts to a plan, such as JSON examples, screenshots, code snippets, or binary reference files. Agents should be able to list, add, delete, and retrieve attachments through MCP without relying on fragile external paths.

Current Code Path / Findings
- Plan persistence is under Helm config: individual plan JSON files in config/plans and dependencies in config/plan-dependencies.json via src/session/persistence.ts.
- getConfigDir() in src/utils/app-paths.ts is the existing source for the config root.
- Existing temp-file flow is temp:writeContent in src/electron/ipc/system-handlers.ts, writing helm-work-* files under the Helm temp dir. Attachment retrieval should follow a similar temp-file return pattern per requirement.
- MCP tool schemas/results live in src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts, with service logic in src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts.
- No attachment model, storage directory, IPC, or MCP tools currently exist.

Proposed Approach
- Add a PlanAttachment metadata model: id, planId, filename, contentType or kind if detectable, size, createdAt, updatedAt, and storagePath/relativePath under Helm config.
- Store attachment files under a config-owned directory such as config/plan-attachments/<planId>/<attachmentId>-<safeFilename>.
- Enforce a 10MB size limit on add.
- Add a small attachment manager/service for path safety, filename sanitization, metadata persistence, and cleanup when plans are deleted if desired.
- Add MCP tools: plan_attachment_list, plan_attachment_add, plan_attachment_delete, plan_attachment_get.
- plan_attachment_list returns filename, size, dates, ids, and maybe contentType.
- plan_attachment_add should accept a filename plus either source temp/path or content/base64/text depending on MCP client constraints; choose a safe contract during implementation.
- plan_attachment_get writes/copies content to a temp file and returns that path, not inline raw/base64 content.
- Decide whether attachment metadata lives inside plan JSON files or a separate attachment index; separate index is likely cleaner for binary-heavy workflows.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- src/types/plan.ts or new src/types/plan-attachment.ts
- src/session/persistence.ts or new src/session/plan-attachment-manager.ts
- src/utils/app-paths.ts if a helper for attachment/temp paths is useful
- src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts
- src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts
- src/electron/ipc/system-handlers.ts if temp-file helper is reused/extracted
- src/electron/ipc/plan-handlers.ts and preload if renderer UI support is included
- tests for attachment manager, MCP tools, size limit, path safety, and temp retrieval

TDD Suggestions
- Test adding JSON/text/image/binary attachment metadata and storage under config dir.
- Test size limit rejects >10MB.
- Test path traversal filenames are sanitized and cannot escape config dir.
- Test list returns filename, size, createdAt/updatedAt, and id.
- Test delete removes metadata and stored file.
- Test get writes/copies to a temp file and returns a path.
- Test missing plan or missing attachment returns clear MCP errors.

Acceptance Criteria
- Attachments support JSON, text/code, images, and arbitrary binary files up to 10MB.
- Attachment files are stored under Helm config dir, not as external references.
- MCP can list, add, delete, and retrieve attachments.
- List includes filenames, size, and dates.
- Get returns a temp file path for the attachment content.
- Storage is path-safe and covered by tests.

Done Statement
Helm plans can own config-stored attachments, and MCP clients can manage and retrieve those attachments via temp files.

items.title: MCP end point for communicating to user via telegram
items.description:
A new mcp endpoint(s) are needed for the llm to communicate to the user via telegram.
the user will reply via telegram to the helm app, who will then paste the content back into the LLM that send out the request. 
similarly, the user can reach out to the telegram bot to identify a session and then message just that session, or create a new sessoin for a new folder.

This would replace the existing telegram operability that we alreaydy have


the LLM needs to say to helm:
- send to user message - should be well instructed to contain only mobile-friendly content. ie, no large wide things
- create channel 
- close channel
- list channels
NB: The llm should only ask via telegram, if telegram is setup, running, and eitehr it has previously been prompted by the user via telegram or they have an utmost blocker that needs clarification asap.

the helm mcp needs to receive from user
- send to session 
- list session

please investigate the practicality and feasiablity of this. and then create a new plan following this one with the plan of your suggestion

items.title: FUTURE: UI display of completion notes
items.description: After core feature ships, optionally add read-only display of completionNotes in plan editor (renderer/plans/plan-editor.ts). Not blocking.

items.title: Document system and update CLAUDE.md
items.description:
## Reviewer Follow-up

Reviewed after DONE. Follow-up bug plan created: P-0098 (Review fix: backup settings must target a selected directory and use documented config format).

Relevant findings:
- docs/plan-backup-restore.md and CLAUDE.md say config/plan-backups.yaml, but PlanBackupManager currently uses JSON.parse/JSON.stringify against that file.
- The follow-up should either make the code accept the documented YAML format or revise the docs/UI to be explicit about JSON.

---

Create docs/plan-backup-restore.md covering:
- Overview (per-directory rolling-window snapshots)
- Configuration (config/plan-backups.yaml format, defaults)
- Restore workflow (open plan-screen → R → select → restore)
- Rolling window example (keep last 10 hours)
- Recovery scenarios (undo changes, restore deleted plans, fix deps)
- Troubleshooting

Update CLAUDE.md section 21 to mention auto-backup + link to new doc

items.title: Plan Backup & Restore System
items.description:
## Reviewer Follow-up

Reviewed after DONE. Follow-up bug plan created: P-0098 (Review fix: backup settings must target a selected directory and use documented config format).

Relevant findings:
- Settings -> Backup Now currently snapshots the first configured directory rather than an explicit/current directory.
- config/plan-backups.yaml is documented as YAML, but PlanBackupManager parses/writes JSON.
- Settings backup failures are silently swallowed.

---

Implement per-directory plan backup/restore with rolling window storage, timestamped snapshots, UI for version selection showing plan count + status, and configurable backup schedule (hours) & rolling window size.

items.title: 6. MCP read terminal output from any session
items.description:
Problem Statement
Helm MCP can send text to sessions and list/get sessions, but one session cannot currently read another session's recent terminal output through MCP. The requested tool should return the last 1..N lines from the bottom, max 100, with both raw and ANSI-stripped output options plus useful metadata.

User POV
As an agent or user coordinating multiple sessions, I want to inspect another session's terminal tail before deciding what to send or whether it is blocked. I need enough metadata to know which session was read, whether the PTY is running, whether the requested line count was clamped, and when output was last seen.

Current Code Path / Findings
- MCP tools are defined in src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts and implemented through src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts.
- Main-process PTY data flows through src/electron/ipc/pty-handlers.ts, which receives pty:data and forwards it to renderers and notification handling.
- Renderer has PtyOutputBuffer in renderer/terminal/pty-output-buffer.ts, but MCP service cannot rely on renderer-only state.
- src/session/notification-manager.ts already maintains an ANSI-stripped output buffer and last lines, but it is oriented toward notifications and not raw output or MCP metadata.
- src/telegram/output-summarizer.ts keeps raw-ish output for Telegram, but it is coupled to Telegram concerns.

Proposed Approach
- Add a main-process session terminal tail buffer owned by session/PTY infrastructure, not renderer UI.
- Feed the buffer from the same PTY data path that currently feeds notificationManager/onPtyData.
- Store enough data for raw and stripped line tails, with a max buffer comfortably above the MCP max 100 lines.
- Add MCP tool session_read_terminal with input: sessionId or name, lines 1..100, mode raw|stripped|both.
- Return metadata: sessionId, name, cliType, workingDir, requestedLines, returnedLines, clamped boolean, maxLines, ptyRunning, lastOutputAt if available, and the selected output arrays.
- Allow reading any session, per requirement, while still requiring MCP authentication.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts
- src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts
- src/session/pty-manager.ts or new src/session/terminal-output-buffer.ts
- src/electron/ipc/pty-handlers.ts
- src/electron/ipc/handlers.ts wiring
- src/session/notification-manager.ts if buffer logic is reused/extracted
- tests/localhost-mcp-server.test.ts
- tests/helm-control-service.test.ts
- new terminal-output-buffer tests or existing pty-output-buffer-inspired tests

TDD Suggestions
- Unit test buffer handling for raw lines, stripped lines, partial lines, CR redraws, and ANSI removal.
- MCP test for clamping lines > 100 and rejecting lines < 1.
- MCP test reading by session id and, if supported, exact session name.
- Test metadata includes PTY running state and lastOutputAt.
- Test unknown session returns a clear error.

Acceptance Criteria
- MCP exposes session_read_terminal.
- Tool can read any active known session by id/name.
- Tool supports raw, stripped, and both output modes.
- Tool clamps to max 100 lines and reports clamping metadata.
- Tool returns session metadata, PTY running state, and last output time when available.

Done Statement
Agents can safely inspect the recent terminal tail of any Helm session through MCP with raw/stripped output and clear metadata.

items.title: 5. Teach agents that P-00xx values are Helm plan IDs
items.description:
Problem Statement
Helm plans already have human-readable IDs like P-0007, but MCP guidance does not clearly teach LLMs that P-00xx refers to a Helm plan. This makes user references less reliable and can cause agents to miss that a short P-id is plan context.

User POV
As a user, I want to be able to say P-0035 and have agents understand that I mean a Helm plan. I should not have to explain every time that P-00xx values are plan IDs.

Current Code Path / Findings
- src/types/plan.ts documents PlanItem.humanId as a human-readable stable identifier for UI/MCP references, e.g. P-0007.
- MCP plan_get currently requires id and the description does not explain human IDs.
- session_info returns available tools and guidance, but does not currently include P-00xx semantics.
- HelmControlService getAvailableTools returns compact tool summaries that also omit P-id education.
- There is no confirmed alias lookup path yet; this plan should investigate whether to document only or support P-id aliases in MCP tools.

Proposed Approach
- Add guidance to session_info explaining that P-00xx values are Helm human-readable plan IDs.
- Update plan-related tool descriptions to tell agents to resolve P-ids with plan_get/plans_list/plans_summary as supported.
- Investigate adding lookup support so MCP tools accepting plan id can accept either canonical UUID id or humanId P-00xx.
- If alias support is added, centralize resolution in HelmControlService/PlanManager rather than ad hoc per-tool logic.
- Ensure ambiguity is handled: human IDs should be treated as globally unique if the current allocator guarantees it; otherwise require directory context on collision.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- src/types/plan.ts
- src/session/plan-manager.ts
- src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts
- src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts
- tests/plan-manager.test.ts
- tests/localhost-mcp-server.test.ts or MCP call tests
- docs/helm-mcp-protocol.md and docs/config-system.md if docs mirror MCP usage

TDD Suggestions
- Test that session_info contains P-00xx explanation.
- Test that plan tool descriptions include humanId/P-id guidance.
- If aliases are implemented, test plan_get and state/link/update/delete tools with humanId as input.
- Test ambiguous/nonexistent humanId behavior gives a clear error.
- Test plans_summary/list expose both canonical id and humanId.

Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can discover from session_info and tool descriptions that P-00xx refers to Helm plans.
- Tool responses continue to expose humanId where available.
- A clear decision is implemented: documentation-only, or humanId aliases accepted by plan tools.
- If aliases are accepted, resolution is shared and covered by tests.

Done Statement
LLMs understand P-00xx references as Helm plan IDs from MCP guidance, and plan tools either clearly document or directly support humanId resolution.

items.title: Write tests for backup/restore system
items.description:
## Reviewer Follow-up

Reviewed after DONE. Follow-up bug plan created: P-0098 (Review fix: backup settings must target a selected directory and use documented config format).

Relevant findings:
- P-0084 requested scheduler tests and validation that setBackupConfig rejects invalid ranges, but tests/plan-backup-handlers.test.ts only verifies updateConfig is called and src/electron/ipc/plan-backup-handlers.ts passes updates through without range validation.
- P-0098 should include adding real validation tests and implementation for maxSnapshots/interval ranges.

---

Create tests/plan-backup-manager.test.ts + extend plan-manager.test.ts:

**PlanBackupManager tests:**
- File I/O: createSnapshot saves correct JSON, listSnapshots reads correctly, pruning keeps N newest
- Restore: loadSnapshot returns complete plans + deps
- Filename encoding: dirPath with special chars (\\, :) properly encoded/decoded
- Edge cases: 0 plans, maxSnapshots=1, empty deps
- Error handling: missing file → null (no throw), corrupt JSON → logged

**Scheduler tests:**
- plan:changed → no snapshot if within interval
- Interval boundary → snapshot created once
- Per-dir overrides respected
- Config disabled → no snapshots
- Manual createBackupNow() bypasses interval

**IPC Handler tests:**
- listBackups returns correct shape
- restoreBackup calls PlanManager.importAll() with correct data
- Validation: setBackupConfig rejects invalid ranges

items.title: 2. First-class plan sequences with swimlane memory
items.description:
Problem Statement
Helm plans are currently individual DAG nodes with dependency connectors, but there is no first-class wrapper for a mission/sequence spanning multiple plans. The requested feature needs a visually visible planner wrapper, likely a swimlane, that contains many plans and gives them shared editable memory.

User POV
As a user planning a larger body of work, I want to group related plans into a visible sequence with a mission statement and shared notes. I want plans inside that sequence to be arranged inside the swimlane so the sequence remains legible in planner mode.

Current Code Path / Findings
- Plan data lives in src/types/plan.ts as PlanItem and PlanDependency only.
- PlanManager in src/session/plan-manager.ts persists individual plan JSON files under config/plans and global dependencies under config/plan-dependencies.json.
- The planner layout is computed by renderer/plans/plan-layout.ts and rendered/managed through renderer/plans/plan-screen.ts.
- plan-screen.ts currently filters by type/status and computes one global layout; it has no sequence/lane grouping concept.
- Existing plan import/export paths will need to preserve any new sequence relationship if sequences become first-class.

Proposed Approach
- Add a first-class PlanSequence model with id, dirPath, title/name, missionStatement, sharedMemory, createdAt, updatedAt, and ordering/display metadata as needed.
- Add a sequenceId field or membership relation for PlanItem.
- Persist sequences under Helm config, preferably separately from individual plan files to avoid bloating every plan file, while keeping imports/exports coherent.
- Extend PlanManager or a sequence manager with create/update/delete/list and assign/unassign plan operations.
- Extend planner layout so sequence members are grouped within a swimlane/wrapper and plan positions are constrained inside that lane.
- Add UI affordances in planner mode to view/edit sequence mission and shared memory from any plan in the sequence.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- src/types/plan.ts
- src/session/plan-manager.ts or new src/session/plan-sequence-manager.ts
- src/session/persistence.ts
- src/electron/ipc/plan-handlers.ts
- src/electron/preload.ts
- renderer/plans/plan-screen.ts
- renderer/plans/plan-layout.ts
- renderer/components/panels/DraftEditor.vue if plan editor exposes sequence memory
- tests/plan-manager.test.ts, tests/plan-screen.test.ts, tests/plan-layout-related coverage

TDD Suggestions
- Model/persistence tests for creating, updating, loading, exporting, and deleting sequences.
- Tests that plan membership survives save/load/import/export.
- Layout tests proving plans in a sequence are grouped into a swimlane and non-members remain outside.
- UI state tests that editing shared memory through one plan is visible from another plan in the same sequence.

Acceptance Criteria
- A sequence is a first-class object, not inferred only from links.
- A sequence has a mission statement and shared memory/notes viewable and editable from all member plans.
- Planner mode visibly wraps member plans in a sequence/swimlane.
- Plans inside a sequence are laid out within that wrapper.
- Existing plan DAG dependency behavior still works inside and outside sequences.

Done Statement
Users can create and edit first-class plan sequences with visible swimlane grouping, shared mission, and shared memory across member plans.

items.title: Schedule tasks UI rework
items.description:
## Reviewer Follow-up

Reviewed after DONE. Follow-up bug plan created: P-0096 (Review fix: scheduled task picker uses wrong CLI API shape and local-time defaults).

Summary of reviewer findings:
- P-0086's implementation maps configGetCliTypes() as Array<{ cliType: string }>, but the live IPC/preload contract returns string[]. This breaks the configured CLI picker.
- The create form does not require a selected CLI and silently falls back to 'claude-code', which violates the configured-CLI picker requirement.
- The default datetime-local value uses toISOString().slice(0, 16), which writes UTC into a local-time control.
- P-0086 asked to see/edit current scheduled tasks; the delivered UI lists and cancels tasks but has no edit path for existing tasks.

P-0096 contains the contextual fix brief and acceptance criteria.

---

scheduled tasks, needs cli picker from teh clis we already have configured.  needs fodler picker from the folders we already have picked. also i fill out all the fields, and still cant save. i need this replaced by a scheduled tasks vue section on the sidebar, like the quick spawn that lets me see/edit the current ones and how much time till execution and also create new ones with the cli and dir popup pickers.

items.title: Fix overview-to-session navigation and active/focused session sync
items.description:
Objective
Fix the intermittent state where the main panel remains in overview mode or shows a terminal that no longer matches the highlighted sidebar session. The fix should make selecting or navigating to a session from overview use one authoritative navigation path, and keep main-view state, terminal-manager active session, state.activeSessionId, and sidebar focus synchronized.

Investigation Summary
The current live path has split ownership:

- renderer/App.vue handles gamepad input while activeView === 'overview'. Its overview block consumes Up/Down to move sessionsState.overviewFocusIndex and returns before sidebar navigation can run. That makes overview behave like a trapped grid.
- renderer/navigation.ts and renderer/screens/group-overview.ts still encode the older contract: overview consumes A/B/Left/Right/X, but Up/Down fall through to handleSessionsScreenButton(), allowing sidebar navigation and autoSelectFocusedSession() to choose a session and exit overview.
- renderer/screens/sessions-spawn.ts::autoSelectFocusedSession() calls navStore.activateSession() and then showTerminalArea(); this can dismiss overview, but only when the input path actually reaches sidebar navigation.
- renderer/stores/navigation.ts has both full navigation (navigateToSession) and thin activation (activateSession). Thin activation intentionally does not clean overlays or sync sidebar focus. That is fine for some flows, but dangerous if overview input uses the wrong route.
- App.vue also mirrors main-view-manager via its own activeView ref and separately forces activeView = 'terminal' from TerminalManager onSwitch. That can hide a view before the main-view transition context has completed unless all session switching goes through the same navigation store contract.
- Existing tests cover navigation-store.navigateToSession dismissing overview, and sessions.ts D-pad focus changes, but they do not cover the Vue App.vue overview input path that currently traps Up/Down and bypasses the old fallthrough behavior.

Likely Root Cause
The Vue overview migration left two behavior contracts active. The user-facing behavior depends on which event path handles the input:

- Vue/App path: overview owns Up/Down, so the user stays in overview unless they press A on an overview card or trigger another flow.
- Legacy path: overview declines Up/Down, sidebar focus moves, auto-select switches the terminal, and overview exits.

This explains the intermittent feel and the displayed-session/sidebar-selection mismatch.

Approach
1. Decide and document the single intended contract for overview input:
   - A on an overview card selects that session via navStore.navigateToSession().
   - B/Escape/Left closes overview and restores the previous terminal/sidebar focus.
   - Right remains consumed while overview is open unless explicitly opening a group from the sidebar.
   - Up/Down should not leave the app stuck. Either they should route to sidebar session navigation when the sidebar is the active focus source, or the app should provide an explicit one-step session selection path that also calls navStore.navigateToSession(). Preserve the existing user expectation that moving to a session can exit overview.
2. Make one module authoritative for overview input semantics. Prefer moving the shared behavior into a small helper in renderer/screens/group-overview.ts or a navigation-store method so App.vue and keyboard/gamepad fallbacks cannot diverge again.
3. Update App.vue to use the shared overview handler instead of duplicating its own incomplete overview block, or make the App.vue block explicitly call the same session-list movement path for Up/Down.
4. Review showTerminalArea()/autoSelectFocusedSession() after the behavior change. If Up/Down selection remains supported, ensure exiting overview uses navStore.navigateToSession() or an equivalent full transition path rather than thin activateSession() plus hideOverview(), unless the thin path is deliberately upgraded to close overlays and sync focus.
5. Keep snapped-out sessions first-class: when the selected session is snapped out, route through navStore.navigateToSession() so sessionSetActive() is used and local terminal switching is not assumed.
6. Keep Vue list ownership intact. Do not reintroduce imperative visual class ownership. updateSessionsFocus() should remain scroll-only; SessionList.vue keeps active/focused props.

Files To Inspect/Change
- renderer/App.vue: overview input block, activeView synchronization, OverviewGrid event wiring.
- renderer/stores/navigation.ts: navigateToSession, activateSession, closeOverview, reconcileTerminalSwitch behavior and comments.
- renderer/screens/group-overview.ts: legacy overview input and restore flags; possible home for shared semantics.
- renderer/screens/sessions-spawn.ts: autoSelectFocusedSession/showTerminalArea interaction.
- renderer/screens/sessions.ts: keyboard fallback routing while currentView() === 'overview'.
- renderer/navigation.ts: legacy gamepad fallback routing; avoid unrelated context-menu dirty changes.
- tests/navigation-store.test.ts, tests/sessions-screen.test.ts, and an App/Vue-level test if available/feasible for the exact OverviewGrid/App.vue input path.

Acceptance Criteria
- From overview mode, moving to/selecting a session reliably exits overview and shows the same session that is highlighted in the sidebar.
- The displayed terminal, state.activeSessionId, TerminalManager active id, and SessionList active/focused row do not diverge after overview selection, D-pad movement, keyboard Arrow movement, overview close/cancel, or quick-spawn open/cancel.
- Overview close without selecting restores the previous terminal and prior sidebar focus.
- Overview card selection still works for both global overview and folder overview.
- Snapped-out sessions still focus the external window instead of attempting a local switch.
- Existing Vue ownership is preserved: no direct DOM class toggling for active/focused session rows.

Verification Plan
- Add/adjust targeted tests for the missing Vue/App overview route if feasible.
- Add/adjust tests proving overview Up/Down or session-list navigation cannot leave activeView/currentView stuck in overview when a session is selected.
- Run targeted suites:
  - npm test -- tests/navigation-store.test.ts
  - npm test -- tests/sessions-screen.test.ts
  - npm test -- tests/group-overview.test.ts
  - npm test -- tests/components/panels/panels.test.ts if OverviewGrid behavior changes
- If a runtime repro is available, manually verify: open global overview, navigate to a sidebar session, select overview card, cancel quick spawn after opening it from overview, and confirm panel/session/sidebar remain aligned.

items.title: 8. Draft autosave keeps one draft identity
items.description:
Problem Statement
Autosave can create duplicate draft chips for a new draft because the editor starts with draftEditorDraftId = null, autosave emits save, and App.vue creates a draft without storing the returned id back into the open editor. Subsequent autosaves therefore create another draft instead of updating the first one.

User POV
As a user writing a draft, I want autosave to protect my work without creating a new chip every time it saves. Once the first autosave/save creates the draft, all later save/apply/delete operations should target that same draft.

Current Code Path / Findings
- DraftManager.create() in src/session/draft-manager.ts already returns the created DraftPrompt with a UUID id.
- renderer/App.vue openDraftEditor() initializes draftEditorDraftId from the existing draft or null for new drafts.
- renderer/components/panels/DraftEditor.vue doAutoSave() emits save for draft mode after changes.
- renderer/App.vue onDraftSave() checks draftEditorDraftId; if null, it calls window.gamepadCli.draftCreate(...) but does not assign the returned id to draftEditorDraftId.
- ChipBar refresh then sees the new draft, while the editor still believes it is unsaved/new, causing duplicate chips on subsequent autosaves.
- Requirement: create only on first autosave/save, then keep the unique id for CRUD; autosave stays enabled for drafts.

Proposed Approach
- Update onDraftSave() so when draftCreate returns a created draft, App.vue stores created.id into draftEditorDraftId while the editor remains open.
- Ensure create return type is correctly exposed through preload/types so TypeScript knows the created draft shape.
- Treat first autosave and explicit Save the same: both create once and then update thereafter.
- Avoid immediately creating an empty draft on opening the editor; creation should happen on first autosave/save with non-empty label/content according to current validation.
- Ensure Apply/Delete after the first autosave uses the captured id and does not leave the saved draft behind accidentally.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- renderer/App.vue
- renderer/components/panels/DraftEditor.vue if save event needs returned-id feedback or save status behavior adjustment
- src/electron/preload.ts draftCreate typing
- src/electron/ipc/draft-handlers.ts if return shape is currently narrowed
- src/session/draft-manager.ts likely no behavior change needed
- tests/components/panels/panels.test.ts
- tests/chip-bar.test.ts or draft/chip integration tests
- tests/draft-submenu.test.ts if draft CRUD assumptions shift

TDD Suggestions
- Test that first autosave for a new draft calls draftCreate once and stores created id.
- Test that second autosave calls draftUpdate with that id, not draftCreate again.
- Test explicit Save after autosave updates/closes rather than creating a duplicate.
- Test Apply after autosave sends text and deletes the saved draft id.
- Test Delete after autosave deletes the saved draft id.

Acceptance Criteria
- A new draft gets a unique id on first successful autosave/save.
- Additional autosaves update the same draft rather than creating duplicate chips.
- Autosave remains enabled for drafts.
- Apply/Delete operate on the captured draft id after autosave.
- Chip bar shows at most one chip for a single open new draft autosave flow.

Done Statement
Draft autosave creates a draft once, retains its id in the open editor, and all subsequent CRUD operations target that same draft.

items.title: 3. Related-plan focus mode with foreground new plans
items.description:
Problem Statement
Planner mode currently supports type/status filtering, but not a focus mode that shows only plans related to the selected plan through dependency connectors. Users need a way to focus on a connected subgraph while still seeing newly-created plans long enough to link them into that related set.

User POV
As a user working in a dense planner graph, I want to select a plan and focus on everything connected to it through all dependency links. Unrelated plans can fade back, but I still need to distinguish unrelated fading from completed-plan dimming. If I create a new plan while focused, I want it to stay visible in the foreground so I can link it; if I refresh/reload without linking it, it can fade back with other unrelated plans.

Current Code Path / Findings
- renderer/plans/plan-screen.ts owns planner state, filters, selection, creation, dependency linking, and reload.
- Current filtering is in matchesFilters(), getFilteredItems(), and getFilteredDeps(), based only on type/status.
- Plan graph dependencies are available in planScreenState.deps.
- Creating a plan happens through createPlan() in plan-screen.ts, which then opens the editor for the created item.
- Existing layout recomputes from filtered items and filtered deps; related-view can either filter layout or dim unrelated nodes while keeping them mounted.

Proposed Approach
- Add related-focus state to planScreenState: active root id, connected id set, and transient foreground ids for new plans created while focus is active.
- Compute connected component by traversing dependencies in both directions from the selected/root plan.
- Prefer dimming unrelated nodes over removing them entirely so graph context remains visible, but add distinct CSS from done/dimmed styling.
- Include transient foreground newly-created plan ids in the focused set until they are linked or until a full planner refresh/reload clears the transient set.
- When a transient plan becomes connected via dependency link, remove it from transient state because it is naturally related.
- Ensure keyboard navigation and selection do not unexpectedly move into background-dimmed unrelated plans unless explicitly allowed.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- renderer/plans/plan-screen.ts
- renderer/plans/plan-layout.ts if layout needs focus-aware positioning
- renderer/styles/main.css or planner CSS module for focus/dim styles
- src/electron/preload.ts only if new IPC is needed, likely not required
- tests/plan-screen.test.ts
- tests/plan-filter.test.ts or new related-focus tests

TDD Suggestions
- Unit test connected-component traversal across direct and multi-hop dependencies.
- Test that unrelated plans get a related-background/dim state distinct from done.
- Test that createPlan while related-focus is active keeps the new plan foreground-visible.
- Test that refresh/reload clears unlinked transient foreground ids.
- Test that linking the new plan makes it part of the connected component naturally.

Acceptance Criteria
- Related means full graph connectivity through all dependency connectors, not only direct edges.
- Unrelated plans are visually faded/dimmed and distinguishable from done plans.
- New plans created during related-focus stay foreground-visible until linked or planner refresh/reload.
- Existing type/status filters continue to work predictably with the related-focus mode.
- Selection/navigation remains coherent in related-focus mode.

Done Statement
Planner mode supports a selected-plan related focus view over the dependency graph, with clear unrelated styling and foreground treatment for newly-created plans awaiting links.

items.title: Implement MCP Telegram user communication channels
items.description:
Problem Statement
Helm has Telegram bot modules, per-session topics, topic input forwarding, and a partial TelegramRelayService, but the MCP surface is not yet a complete channel model for LLM-to-user communication. Agents need deliberate, mobile-friendly Telegram requests to the user, channel creation/list/close operations, and reliable user replies pasted back into the originating session.

User POV
As a user, I want an agent to ask me concise Telegram questions only when Telegram is configured/running and the question is urgent or I have already engaged through Telegram. I should be able to reply from Telegram and have Helm route my answer back to the exact LLM session that asked. I should also be able to use the bot to find sessions, message a chosen session, or create a session for a configured folder without brittle manual routing.

Done Statement
Helm exposes a complete MCP-backed Telegram communication workflow: agents can list/create/close communication channels, send mobile-friendly messages to the user, receive user replies back into the originating session, and Telegram users can list sessions, send to a selected session, or create a session in a configured folder.

Files / Classes Affected
- src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts
- src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts
- src/telegram/relay-service.ts
- src/telegram/orchestrator.ts
- src/telegram/topic-input.ts
- src/telegram/topic-manager.ts
- src/telegram/commands.ts
- src/telegram/callback-handler.ts
- src/telegram/keyboards.ts
- src/telegram/bot.ts
- tests/localhost-mcp-server.test.ts
- tests/helm-control-service.test.ts
- tests/topic-input.test.ts
- new or expanded Telegram relay/channel tests

Current Code Path / Findings
- TelegramBotCore already handles start/stop, allowed user filtering, commands, callback queries, forum-topic sending, and topic creation/close/edit helpers.
- TopicManager already maps sessions to Telegram forum topics via SessionInfo.topicId and persists that mapping through sessions.yaml.
- setupTopicInput currently forwards user text directly to a mapped session/topic or active session via PtyManager, which works for general Telegram-to-session input but does not preserve an MCP reply correlation token.
- TelegramRelayService already exists and is wired from HelmControlService events in orchestrator.ts, but it is incomplete as a first-class MCP contract. It keeps pending reply tokens and can send a deliberate session reply to Telegram, yet inbound Telegram messages are not consistently routed through it.
- HelmControlService currently exposes telegram_send and telegram_set_output_mode only. It emits events instead of owning typed channel lifecycle APIs.
- LocalhostMcpServer exposes telegram_send and telegram_set_output_mode, but no create/list/close channel tools and no status guard that tells an agent whether Telegram is configured and running.
- TelegramRelayService currently calls sendTextToSession with extra metadata not present in the service method type, so part of the intended design is not contractually captured by tests.

Approach
- Define a Telegram channel model in the service layer. A channel should identify the originating session, Telegram topic or chat context, optional pending reply token, status, createdAt/updatedAt, and whether it expects a response.
- Add MCP tools with schemas and descriptions:
  - telegram_status: report configured/running/available state, active chat/topic context, and whether agent-initiated asks are allowed.
  - telegram_channel_list: list open channels and their mapped session/topic state.
  - telegram_channel_create: create or reuse a channel for a session and ensure a Telegram topic exists when possible.
  - telegram_channel_close: close or expire a channel without deleting unrelated session topics unless explicitly requested.
  - telegram_send_to_user: send concise mobile-friendly text from a session/channel to Telegram, optionally expecting a reply.
- Keep telegram_send as a compatibility wrapper or migrate it to telegram_send_to_user with clear naming.
- Route Telegram replies through TelegramRelayService before falling back to direct topic input. If a reply references an open/pending channel, paste it back to the originating session using HelmControlService.sendTextToSession or PtyManager delivery with a HELM_MSG envelope.
- Preserve existing slash-command and topic-send behavior while making MCP channels the deliberate ask/reply path.
- Add guardrails: reject agent sends when Telegram is not configured/running; include tool descriptions that instruct agents to keep content mobile-friendly and use Telegram only after user Telegram engagement or an urgent blocker.
- Add rate limiting or size checks for outbound Telegram text so wide/long payloads are rejected or summarized before sending.
- Avoid storing bot tokens or sensitive config in MCP responses.

TDD Suggestions
- Unit test MCP tool listing and dispatch for telegram_status, channel list/create/close, and send-to-user.
- Unit test service guard behavior when Telegram is disabled, missing chatId, missing allowed users, or bot is stopped.
- Unit test creating/reusing a channel for an existing session topic.
- Unit test user reply routing: Telegram message with pending channel token is delivered back to the original session and clears or updates pending state.
- Regression test that ordinary topic messages still route to mapped sessions when no pending MCP channel owns the reply.
- Unit test mobile-friendly text validation and error messages for oversized/wide content.
- Unit test that TelegramRelayService no longer passes untyped metadata to sendTextToSession.

Acceptance Criteria
- MCP clients can query Telegram availability before sending.
- MCP clients can create, list, close, and send through Telegram communication channels.
- Agent-facing tool descriptions clearly instruct mobile-friendly, rare/urgent use.
- User replies from Telegram are routed back into the LLM session that asked, with correlation preserved.
- Existing Telegram session/topic commands continue to work.
- No sensitive Telegram token or private config value is exposed through MCP responses.
- Tests cover MCP schemas/dispatch, channel lifecycle, reply routing, unavailable Telegram states, and compatibility with existing topic input.

items.title: Implement mandatory plan completion documentation
items.description:
Add completionNotes field to PlanItem type and require documentation when marking plans done.

CHANGES:
1. src/types/plan.ts: Add completionNotes?: string to PlanItem
2. src/session/plan-manager.ts: Update completeItem(id, documentation) to store docs before marking done
3. src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts: Pass documentation through completePlan(id, documentation)
4. src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts: Update plan_complete tool schema - add required 'documentation' param (min 10 chars), validate non-empty, update handler

TESTS:
- plan-manager.test.ts: completeItem appends docs, validates non-empty, persists
- localhost-mcp-server.test.ts: plan_complete rejects missing/empty docs
- persistence.test.ts: backwards compatibility with old plans

Run: npm test to verify full suite passes, no regressions

items.title: Build restore modal + settings panel integration
items.description:
## Reviewer Follow-up

Reviewed after DONE. Follow-up bug plan created: P-0098 (Review fix: backup settings must target a selected directory and use documented config format).

Relevant findings:
- BackupTab's manual Backup Now uses the first configured directory rather than a selected/current directory.
- BackupTab silently ignores backup/config save errors.
- The documented config/plan-backups.yaml format does not match PlanBackupManager's JSON.parse/JSON.stringify implementation.
- BackupRestoreModal does not provide the requested selected-snapshot preview/diff details.
- Restore success currently shows a generic notice instead of identifying the restored timestamp.

---

1. **Restore Modal (renderer/plans/backup-restore-modal.ts):**
   - Vue 3 component showing dated backups (newest first, scrollable)
   - Each row: \"2024-04-28 09:30\" · \"3 plans · 2 deps\" · status badge (✓/⚠/✗)
   - Click/Select to preview (show timestamp, plan count, age, diff vs current)
   - Buttons: Restore (IPC call) | Delete | Cancel
   - Manual \"Backup Now\" button at bottom
   - Arrow keys to navigate, A=confirm, B=close, Backspace=delete
   - Error handling + loading state

2. **Plan Screen Integration:**
   - Add 🗚 Restore button in header → opens modal
   - Keyboard shortcut: R key to open
   - After restore: reload plan data + show \"Restored from {timestamp}\" notice
   - Add modal via Teleport (same pattern as plan help modal)

3. **Settings Panel Integration:**
   - New collapsible section \"Plan Backups\"
   - Toggles/sliders: enable | maxSnapshots (1-100) | interval (1-168 hrs)
   - \"Backup Now\" manual trigger button
   - Debounced config save on change
   - Show disk usage estimate (if available)

items.title: 4. Explicit plan ownership and MCP working-plan reminders
items.description:
Problem Statement
MCP should remind LLMs to mark plans as coding and set the session working-plan flag when they start work, but it should not silently do that for them. Separately, there appears to be a bug where editing a plan can assign or preserve session ownership unexpectedly.

User POV
As a user supervising multiple agents, I want session/plan ownership to be intentional and visible. Editing a plan should not accidentally make the current session own it. When an LLM starts implementation, Helm should remind it to explicitly claim the plan instead of guessing for it.

Current Code Path / Findings
- MCP tool descriptions/results live in src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts and src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts.
- session_set_working_plan in HelmControlService explicitly assigns a plan to a session and moves it to coding when allowed.
- plan_set_state validates coding needs a sessionId unless already assigned.
- PlanManager.setState() keeps existing sessionId for planning/ready/review/blocked unless a new sessionId is supplied: item.sessionId = sessionId ?? item.sessionId.
- renderer/plans/plan-screen.ts handleSave() can call planSetState(selectedId, updates.status, updates.stateInfo, targetSessionId ?? current.sessionId). That fallback is suspicious for unwanted assignment when editing state from the UI.
- Requirement: assignment should be explicit only. Review/blocked should keep existing owner by default.

Proposed Approach
- Add MCP post-call reminder text for plan_create and plan_set_state results, and strengthen tool descriptions: when starting work, call plan_set_state(... coding, sessionId) or session_set_working_plan explicitly, then set AIAGENT state.
- Do not auto-call session_set_working_plan from plan_create or plan_set_state.
- Audit plan editor save path so merely editing a title/description/status does not assign current session unless the user explicitly applies/claims the plan.
- Preserve existing owner for review/blocked by default, but avoid injecting a current session fallback into previously unowned plans.
- Clarify ownership transitions in PlanManager.setState and/or the renderer caller so the model is explicit and testable.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts
- src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts
- src/session/plan-manager.ts
- renderer/plans/plan-screen.ts
- renderer/drafts/draft-editor.ts and renderer/components/panels/DraftEditor.vue if status editing emits ownership data
- tests/plan-manager.test.ts
- tests/plan-screen.test.ts
- tests/localhost-mcp-server.test.ts or helm-control-service MCP tests

TDD Suggestions
- Test that editing title/description does not set sessionId.
- Test that changing an unowned plan to review/blocked from editor does not assign current active session.
- Test that review/blocked keep an existing owner when no explicit new owner is supplied.
- Test that coding still requires explicit sessionId or existing owner.
- Test that MCP plan_create/plan_set_state responses include reminder guidance but do not mutate session currentPlanId.

Acceptance Criteria
- MCP reminds LLMs to explicitly set working plan / coding state when starting work.
- No automatic ownership assignment is added to plan_create or plan_set_state beyond existing explicit parameters.
- Editing a plan never assigns it to a session unless the user or agent explicitly applies/claims it.
- Review and blocked preserve existing owner by default.
- Regression tests cover the unwanted assignment path.

Done Statement
Plan ownership is explicit and predictable: MCP nudges agents, but only explicit apply/working-plan/coding-with-session flows assign session ownership.

items.title: 1. MCP LLM plan guidance and linked questions
items.description:
Problem Statement
LLMs using Helm MCP need explicit guidance about when to create a new plan, what information the plan must contain, and how to handle questions. Current MCP plan guidance is sparse: plan_create is described as only creating a plan item, plan_complete requires documentation, and session_info focuses on MCP/AIAGENT/session basics rather than plan quality or linked question behavior.

User POV
As a user coordinating multiple LLM sessions, I want agents to create high-quality Helm plans with enough context to be actionable later. If an agent is blocked by a question, I want that question represented as a separate plan linked to the original task, rather than buried in prose or overwriting the original plan.

Current Code Path / Findings
- MCP tool schemas/descriptions live in src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts.
- session_info response and available tool summaries are built in src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts.
- plan_create currently accepts dirPath/title/description/type but does not instruct agents on required content.
- plan_complete currently requires documentation, but the guidance does not define a useful done statement shape.
- Link operations already exist as plan_nextplan_link / plan_nextplan_unlink, so question plans can be represented as separate linked plans without inventing a new dependency mechanism.

Proposed Approach
- Add an agent planning guide to session_info.
- Expand plan_create, plan_complete, plan_get, plans_summary, plan_nextplan_link, and relevant available_tools descriptions so agents see the guidance both before and after startup.
- Add reminder text to plan_create results if feasible, without changing the created PlanItem schema unless needed.
- Define that question plans should be separate Helm plans linked to the original task.
- Define required plan description sections: Problem Statement, User POV, Done Statement, Files / Classes Affected, TDD Suggestions, and Acceptance Criteria.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- src/mcp/localhost-mcp-server.ts
- src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts
- tests/localhost-mcp-server.test.ts or MCP tool-description tests if present
- tests/helm-control-service.test.ts
- docs/config-system.md and/or docs/helm-mcp-protocol.md if MCP docs mirror tool behavior

TDD Suggestions
- Add assertions that session_info includes the new plan guidance.
- Add assertions that tool descriptions mention required plan sections and question-link behavior.
- Add a regression test that plan_create still returns valid structuredContent after added reminders.
- Add a test that linked question guidance references plan_nextplan_link, not ad hoc text-only references.

Acceptance Criteria
- session_info tells LLMs when to create new plans and how to structure them.
- Relevant tool descriptions/results repeat enough guidance that agents using tool discovery alone still see it.
- Guidance explicitly says questions become separate plans linked to the original.
- Required sections are listed exactly enough to be followed consistently.
- MCP output remains valid record-shaped structuredContent.

Done Statement
Agents using Helm MCP can discover and follow plan-quality guidance from both session_info and tool descriptions/results, and question follow-ups are documented as separate linked plans.

items.title: 9. Preserve dependency chains when deleting plans
items.description:
Problem Statement
Deleting a plan currently removes all dependency edges touching that plan. For a chain like A -> B -> C, deleting B leaves A and C disconnected. The requested behavior is to preserve ordering by connecting all incoming predecessors to all outgoing successors when a middle plan is deleted.

User POV
As a user pruning a plan graph, I want deleting an intermediate plan to maintain the intended sequence where possible. If B is removed from A -> B -> C, I expect A -> C to remain so the workflow order is not lost.

Current Code Path / Findings
- PlanManager.delete(id) in src/session/plan-manager.ts deletes the plan file, removes the item, filters out dependencies touching the id, recomputes startable state, and saves.
- Existing addDependency() and wouldCreateCycle() already enforce no self-links, no cross-directory links, no duplicates, and acyclic DAG behavior.
- Dependency persistence is global in config/plan-dependencies.json via src/session/persistence.ts.
- UI deletion flows route through plan handlers/plan-screen requestDelete and ultimately PlanManager.delete.
- Requirement: apply to all deletes, crossing all incoming parents with all outgoing children, while respecting existing DAG/cycle rules.

Proposed Approach
- Before removing edges for the deleted plan, collect incoming dependencies where toId === deletedId and outgoing dependencies where fromId === deletedId.
- Remove the deleted plan and all touching edges.
- For every incoming.fromId x outgoing.toId pair, attempt to add a direct dependency if not self, same directory, not duplicate, and not cycle-producing.
- Reuse or extract existing dependency validation/cycle logic so delete-chain preservation follows the same DAG rules as addDependency.
- Record/log skipped links for duplicates/cycles/self-links; user-facing notice is optional but tests should verify safe behavior.
- Recompute startable state and persist the updated dependency list once.

Files / Classes Likely Affected
- src/session/plan-manager.ts
- src/session/persistence.ts likely unchanged except tests
- src/electron/ipc/plan-handlers.ts only if delete result should report rewired/skipped counts
- renderer/plans/plan-screen.ts only if UI should display a notice
- tests/plan-manager.test.ts
- tests/plan-persistence.test.ts or plan-screen deletion tests

TDD Suggestions
- Test A -> B -> C, delete B results in A -> C.
- Test multiple parents and children produce all valid cross-product links.
- Test duplicate candidate links are not duplicated.
- Test cycle-producing candidates are skipped according to existing DAG rules.
- Test cross-directory links are not created.
- Test recomputeStartable still updates affected successors.

Acceptance Criteria
- Every delete preserves valid ordering by connecting all incoming predecessors to all outgoing successors.
- The feature applies to all plan delete paths.
- Existing DAG safety rules are preserved: no cycles, no duplicate edges, no cross-directory edges, no self-edges.
- Plan dependencies persist correctly after deletion and reload.
- Tests cover simple chain and multi-parent/multi-child cases.

Done Statement
Deleting an intermediate plan rewires valid dependency chains automatically while preserving Helm's DAG constraints.

sequences.title: Fix plan status mismatch: P-0051 filter persistence requirement not met in original delivery Sequence
sequences.missionStatement:
## What Has Been Done

Investigated and documented the relationship between P-0051 and P-0055 regarding filter persistence.

**Findings:**
1. P-0051 (commit ea0b569) delivered filtering UI and logic but WITHOUT persistence
2. P-0055 (commit 361f785) delivered persistence later as a separate plan
3. P-0051 was marked review/done without meeting its original acceptance criteria

**Resolution:**
Updated both P-0051 and P-0055 descriptions to properly document their relationship:
- P-0051 now acknowledges that persistence was completed by P-0055
- P-0055 now explicitly states that it completes P-0051's persistence requirement
- Both plan descriptions clearly state that together they deliver the complete filtering feature

**Status:**
The filter persistence requirement is now fully implemented and properly documented. P-0051 + P-0055 together satisfy the original requirement: "Filters persist across close/reopen. Default: show all."

No code changes were required - this was a documentation/process fix to properly reflect the work that was already done.

---

## Issue

P-0051 (Add filtering to plan screen) had explicit requirement: "Filters persist across close/reopen. Default: show all." The original delivery (commit ea0b569) only implemented in-memory filter state, not disk persistence.

The requirement was technically met later by P-0055, but P-0051 was marked done/review without fulfilling its original acceptance criteria.

## Impact

- P-0051 should have been blocked until persistence was implemented
- Acceptance criteria says "persist across close/reopen" but initial delivery violated this
- P-0055 (persistence) was created as separate work instead of being part of P-0051

## What Needs Fixing

Review P-0051's final acceptance and either:
1. Mark P-0051 as incomplete and move its original requirements into P-0055, OR
2. Verify P-0051 was actually completed with persistence (check commit ea0b569 fully)

If option 1: verify P-0055 is marked as completing P-0051's original requirements.

sequences.title: Build restore modal + settings panel integration Sequence
sequences.missionStatement:
## Reviewer Follow-up

Reviewed after DONE. Follow-up bug plan created: P-0098 (Review fix: backup settings must target a selected directory and use documented config format).

Relevant findings:
- BackupTab's manual Backup Now uses the first configured directory rather than a selected/current directory.
- BackupTab silently ignores backup/config save errors.
- The documented config/plan-backups.yaml format does not match PlanBackupManager's JSON.parse/JSON.stringify implementation.
- BackupRestoreModal does not provide the requested selected-snapshot preview/diff details.
- Restore success currently shows a generic notice instead of identifying the restored timestamp.

---

1. **Restore Modal (renderer/plans/backup-restore-modal.ts):**
   - Vue 3 component showing dated backups (newest first, scrollable)
   - Each row: \"2024-04-28 09:30\" · \"3 plans · 2 deps\" · status badge (✓/⚠/✗)
   - Click/Select to preview (show timestamp, plan count, age, diff vs current)
